Import torch, numpy, and the necessary modules from transformers (RobertaConfig, RobertaModel), and the DeepSpeed get_accelerator function
Set a fixed random seed for torch, numpy, and random modules (e.g., torch.manual_seed(42), np.random.seed(0), random.seed(0)) to ensure reproducibility

Initialize a RobertaConfig with parameters matching those in create_model (hidden_size=256, num_attention_heads=8, num_hidden_layers=6, pad_token_id=1, bos_token_id=0, eos_token_id=2, vocab_size=50265, max_position_embeddings=514)
Instantiate a RobertaModel encoder using this configuration
Create a RobertaMLMModel instance using the RobertaConfig and RobertaModel encoder

Create dummy input tensors representing a batch of token sequences with batch_size=2 and seq_len=10, where the tokens include both regular tokens and an eos token (token id 2) placed near the end (e.g., tokens: [[0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 2, 1, 1], [0, 7, 12, 17, 22, 27, 32, 2, 1, 1]]), where 1 is pad_token_id
Create corresponding attention masks where attention_mask is 1 for tokens != pad_token_id and 0 otherwise
Create target token tensors (tgt_tokens) of the same shape, with some tokens masked (using mask_token_id which for RoBERTa is 50264) and including the eos token at the same position as in src_tokens

Define an action_mask tensor of shape (batch_size, seq_len), where tokens that should contribute to the loss are marked with 1, and tokens to be masked out are 0; ensure the eos token position is set to 1 (i.e., not masked out)
Set start index to 1 or 2 (choose start=1 for this test) to simulate the slicing in actor_loss calculation

Manually compute slices of actor_log_prob, log_probs, advantages, and action_mask tensors along dimension 1 using the bugged slicing: action_mask[:, start:] (so eos token is excluded if it falls before or at the start index)
Repeat the above but also compute slices using the proposed correct slicing: action_mask[:, start-1:-1], which includes the eos token
Call the actor_loss function (or a mock loss function simulating actor_loss_fn) with these sliced tensors and record the resulting loss values

Assert that when using the bugged slicing (action_mask[:, start:]), the eos token's contribution to the loss is filtered out (verify by checking that positions corresponding to eos token have zero mask or zero contribution in loss)
Assert that when using the corrected slicing (action_mask[:, start-1:-1]), the eos token's contribution is included (verify by checking non-zero mask values at eos position and contribution to loss)
Print or log the difference in loss values between the two slicing strategies to highlight the effect of masking out eos token
Optionally, verify that the ground reward associated with the eos token is missing in the loss when using the bugged slicing by comparing advantage or reward vectors aligned with the mask
Ensure all tensors are on CPU (or the same device) to avoid device mismatch errors during slicing and loss computation
Wrap the above steps in a test function or script that can be run standalone to reproduce the bug reliably
